questions.question: What is the '5-7 Touch Sweet Spot' for B2B outreach sequences?

options.text: Send 5-7 emails on the first day for maximum impact
80% of B2B replies come from touches 2-5; beyond touch 7, you add less than 5% incremental replies while increasing annoyance risk
Always send exactly 7 touches regardless of response
5-7 different channels should be used simultaneously

questions.explanation: Data from 2025-2026 shows 80% of replies come from touches 2-5. Touch 1 gets ~30%, Touch 2 ~25%, Touches 3-5 ~25% combined. Beyond Touch 7, incremental replies drop below 5% while annoyance and unsubscribe risk increases significantly.

questions.question: What were the three critical errors in Sarah's $47K multi-channel sequence failure?

options.text: Wrong tools, bad timing, high cost
Too many touches too fast, no channel logic (wrong order), and generic AI personalization
Not enough volume, wrong ICP, missing CTA
Using email instead of phone, skipping LinkedIn, no follow-ups

questions.explanation: Sarah made three errors: too many touches too fast (7 in 21 days across 3 channels felt like harassment), no channel logic (LinkedIn connection request before prospects even knew who she was), and generic personalization (AI lines referencing 'your company's growth' that apply to everyone).

questions.question: For a solo founder doing less than 500 emails/week on a budget under $50/month, which sequence archetype is recommended?
questions.explanation: The Email-Only archetype (5 touches over 21 days) is best for low-to-mid ticket deals, high-volume outreach, and budget-conscious solo founders under $50/month in tools. Master email before adding channel complexity.

questions.question: Multi-channel outreach done wrong is worse than email-only outreach done right.
questions.explanation: True. More channels mean more ways to make mistakes. A poorly designed multi-channel sequence can get your LinkedIn banned, damage your email domain, and burn through prospects permanently. The lesson emphasizes mastering one channel (email) before adding complexity.

questions.question: Design a 5-touch email sequence for your specific ICP. For each touch, describe the timing (which day), the angle (problem, case study, value-add, social proof, or breakup), and one example subject line. Explain why you ordered the touches this way.
questions.aiPrompt: Evaluate whether the response includes all 5 touches with specific timing, distinct angles for each (not repeating the same approach), and example subject lines. Look for logical progression (problem-first opener, different angles in follow-ups, warm breakup at the end) and appropriate spacing between touches.
